{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.2": 4, "R1.3": 3, "R1.6": 5, "RX.1": 1, "RX.3": 3, "RX.4": 5, "RX.5": 1},
  "rationale": {"R1.2": "Identifies a key inconsistency (train/deploy gap) and proposes a fix (coupling).", "R1.3": "Doesn't explicitly link claims to evidence in the revised text.", "R1.6": "Reads as a carefully edited pass with consistent register and no loss of meaning.", "RX.1": "Does not mark confidence levels or missing evidence for any recommendations.", "RX.3": "Mostly within scope, but the original abstract may have covered more ground.", "RX.4": "No regressions identified; avoids introducing new errors.", "RX.5": "Major rewrites are not traced back to sources."},
  "confidence": {"R1.2": 0.9, "R1.3": 0.7, "R1.6": 0.95, "RX.1": 0.8, "RX.3": 0.6, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.6},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.2: Identifies a key inconsistency (train/deploy gap) and proposes a fix (coupling).
rationale.R1.3: Doesn't explicitly link claims to evidence in the revised text.
rationale.R1.6: Reads as a carefully edited pass with consistent register and no loss of meaning.
rationale.RX.1: Does not mark confidence levels or missing evidence for any recommendations.
rationale.RX.3: Mostly within scope, but the original abstract may have covered more ground.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions identified; avoids introducing new errors.
rationale.RX.5: Major rewrites are not traced back to sources.
